question: Will CPI rise more than 1.0% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI report has already been released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics. The data shows CPI increased 0.3% in March on a seasonally adjusted basis, which is significantly below the 1.0% threshold needed for YES resolution. This means the market will definitively resolve to NO, making the true probability 0%.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows March 2026 CPI increased only 0.3% month-over-month
Market resolution criteria requires more than 1.0% increase
Data has been officially released and confirmed by multiple sources
Market closes tomorrow but outcome is already determined
risks: Potential data revision by BLS (though highly unlikely to increase from 0.3% to over 1.0%)
Misinterpretation of resolution criteria (though criteria clearly states single-decimal CPI increase)
